role_title: Head of Revenue Strategy and Operations - AMER

location.office_time_required: Monday, Tuesday, and Thursday in-office (standard); Wednesday and Friday variable

seniority.level: Senior Director / VP (people manager, global scope)
level_signals: 10+ years of experience in strategy, operations, or similar roles
Experience building and leading multi-layered teams across strategy and operational functions
Regular exposure to senior leadership and C-suite; trusted advisor to GMs
Comp range up to $316,000 base (Zone A), consistent with VP/Senior Director level
Described as 'one of the most senior roles on the Revenue Strategy & Operations team'

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: 10+ years of experience in strategy, operations, or similar roles, ideally within a software or high-growth enterprise environment.
Experience building and leading multi-layered teams across strategy and operational functions.
Proven track record owning both strategic planning and day-to-day operational execution.
Energized by building in white space; the Velocity remit is an emerging priority that will need structure, strategy, and operational foundation built around an evolving GTM motion.
Demonstrated ability to work cross-functionally and act as a trusted advisor to executive stakeholders, including GMs and C-suite leaders.
Exceptional analytical fluency and communication skills, with the range to move between high-level strategy and ground-level execution.
Demonstrates curiosity about AI tools and emerging technologies, with a willingness to learn and leverage them to enhance productivity, collaboration, or decision-making.

nice_to_haves.raw_text_from_jd: 10+ years of experience in strategy, operations, or similar roles, ideally within a software or high-growth enterprise environment.
Familiarity with AI-powered GTM tooling and a point of view on where automation creates leverage in a revenue org.

scope.surface: AMER Revenue Strategy & Operations + Global Velocity GTM motion (SDR, BDR, SMB)
scope.business_model: B2B SaaS, enterprise and SMB segments
primary_metrics_inferred: Revenue growth across AMER and Velocity
cross_functional_partners: Global Head of Revenue Strategy and Operations
core_responsibilities: Define Asana's go-to-market strategy for the AMER region over a 1–3 year horizon, acting as a strategic partner to the GM of AMER across both Enterprise and Corporate segments.
Own AMER operational execution: forecasting cadence, pipeline health, QBRs, and performance readouts with clearly explained variance and recommended actions.
Lead strategy and operations for Asana's global Velocity business, building the operating model, planning inputs, and execution infrastructure for this motion as it scales.
Embed AI-native tooling and workflows into AMER and Velocity operating rhythms, driving measurable improvements in AE productivity, pipeline velocity, and forecast accuracy.
Translate annual strategy into detailed plans during planning season, including territory and coverage design, book-of-business allocation, headcount recommendations, and productivity targets.
Identify, own, and execute strategic initiatives to drive growth and efficiency across AMER and Velocity.
Drive data-driven decision-making and surface proactive insights; own the operating cadence that keeps the business informed and moving.
Build, develop, and retain a high-performing team.
Partner closely with the Global Head of Revenue Strategy and Operations on org-wide priorities.

green_flags: Compensation range explicitly disclosed across three geographic pay zones ($215K–$316K base)
Equity mentioned as part of total compensation
Clear scope of role — both AMER regional ownership and global Velocity remit are explicitly described
High-visibility role with regular exposure to senior leadership and C-suite
Explicitly encourages applications from candidates who don't meet every listed requirement
Remote eligibility within the US depending on state
Asana named to Fortune's Best Workplaces for 7+ years
red_flags: Dual remit (AMER region + global Velocity) may be very broad scope for a single leader
Velocity business described as 'emerging priority' — significant ambiguity and build-from-scratch expected
Work auth and visa sponsorship not stated anywhere in the JD
Remote eligibility is state-dependent and not fully clarified in the JD
JD tagged #LI-Hybrid but body allows full remote for some US states — mild inconsistency

fields_inferred_not_stated: seniority.level (inferred from comp range, scope, and 'one of the most senior roles' language)
scope.primary_metrics_inferred (inferred from responsibilities; not directly listed in JD)
scope.business_model (inferred from product context and GTM motion description)
compensation.base_range_usd min/max (derived by combining all three geographic zones; Zone A $269K–$316K, Zone B $242K–$284K, Zone C $215K–$253K — range reflects overall floor and ceiling)
ambiguities: JD is tagged #LI-Hybrid but explicitly states 'This role can either be fully remote depending on which US state you live in' — treated as hybrid with remote-US eligibility depending on state.
Work authorization and visa sponsorship are not mentioned anywhere; classified as 'unclear'.

The 'ideally within a software or high-growth enterprise environment' qualifier on the 10+ years requirement makes it ambiguous whether this is a hard or soft requirement. Treated as soft (nice-to-have) while the 10+ years itself is hard.
Equity form is mentioned but not specified (options, RSUs, etc.).
